etymology.description: 山(山) (shān, "mountain") + 幺(幺) (yāo, "tiny; short") = mountain with a narrow, lofty height -> high and steep
etymology.interpretation: 崾(崾) (yǎo, "high and steep") combines 山(山) (shān, "mountain") as the semantic element, representing the landscape, and 幺(幺) (yāo, "tiny; short") for its phonetic contribution, suggesting the narrowness or steepness of the terrain. This combination reflects the concept of a high and steep mountain.
